title: Quiz: Scaling Trapped Ions
description: Test your understanding of scaling challenges

questions.question: What is mode crowding in trapped ion systems?

options.text: Ions physically getting too close together
Motional mode frequencies becoming too closely spaced to address individually

questions.explanation: Mode crowding refers to the motional mode frequencies bunching together as more ions are added, making it difficult to address specific modes for gates without affecting others.

questions.question: What is the QCCD (Quantum Charge Coupled Device) approach?

options.text: Using classical CCDs to image ion positions
Shuttling ions between different zones for computation and storage

questions.explanation: QCCD architecture uses segmented traps where ions are physically shuttled between interaction zones (for gates) and storage zones, allowing modular scaling.

questions.question: What is a major challenge with photonic interconnects between ion traps?

options.text: The speed of light is too slow
Very low success probability per entanglement attempt
It only works at room temperature

questions.explanation: Photonic interconnects have low success probability (~10^-6 per attempt) due to collection efficiency, fiber coupling, and detector efficiency, requiring many attempts for each entangled pair.
